observations.recommendation:
We reiterated and Management agreed with the following recommendations:
a. The Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to record major repairs and replacements in CY 2023 and prior years to the carrying amounts of items of PPE with provisions for depreciation and/or impairment using the following pro-forma entry: Account Debit Credit Property, Plant and Equipment xxx Prior Period Adjustments xxx Accumulated Depreciation xxx Accumulated Impairment Losses xxx To record major repairs and replacements in CY 2023 and prior years to the carrying amounts of items of PPE with provisions for depreciation and impairment.
b. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to record the derecognition of replaced PPE using the following pro-forma entry: Account Debit Credit Other Supplies & Materials Inventory xxx Accumulated Depreciation xxx Property, Plant and Equipment xxx To record derecognition of replaced PPE and recognize its scrap value.
c. The Municipal Budget Officer, in the preparation of future executive budgets, see to it that budgets for major repairs and replacements of PPE, and in case of procured spare parts amounting to ₱50,000.00 or more per unit, be charged out of the capital outlay appropriations and not under the MOOE appropriations. Budgetary accounts be unified and harmonized with the accounts used in the recording of financial transactions in the books.
d. Prospectively, the Municipal Accountant record major repairs and replacements of parts of PPE in accordance with the recognition principles provided in IPSAS 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant for accounting/recording purposes, transfer all receipts pertaining to the 80% SNW to the separate depository bank account created for the purpose as a good control mechanism and to minimize the risk that cash pertaining to 80% SNW may be utilized for other purposes that what it is specifically intended to, i.e., for local development and livelihood projects or lowering the cost of electricity in the locality.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Record all valid reconciling items appearing in the BRSs that require adjustments and/or corrections in the books.
b. Secure supporting documents of the unrecorded check disbursements totaling ₱16.978 million so they may likewise be dropped as reconciling items in the BRS and recovered or can hardly be reconstructed due to transactions were as early as 2013.
c. Cancel stale checks and prepare adjusting/correcting entries to restore the amounts to the Cash in Bank account. For checks which became stale in the hands of the payee that require replacement, issue a new check upon submission of the stale check and other necessary documents and prepare the appropriate accounting entries.

We reiterated our recommendation that the members of the Local Finance Committee, the Municipal Energization Head and the Municipal Accountant see to it that only projects or their work components that may qualify to the requirements set by the provisions of Section 294 of RA No. 7160, Section 66 of RA No. 9136 and its IRR and Section 2.4(a) of DILG-DOE Circular No. 98-01 on the utilization of the Share from the National Wealth be included in plans and budgets of, and be implemented and charged against the 80% component of the Share from the National Wealth of the Municipality.

We recommended that the designated Municipal Tourism Officer, with the support of Municipal Tourism, Culture and Arts Council, revise and update the Local Tourism Development Plan, in the format suggested by the Department of Local Tourism’s 2017 Revised Tourism Guidebook for Local Government Units, reflecting the current tourism strategies of the Municipality, harmonizing it with the other existing municipal plans and budgets and incorporating monitoring and evaluation strategies as means to measure success indicators and justify tourism-related expenditures.

We recommended that the members of the Local Finance Committee, the Municipal Energization Head and the Municipal Accountant see to it that only projects or their work components that may qualify to the requirements set by the provisions of Section 294 of RA No. 7160, Section 66 of RA No. 9136 and its IRR and Section 2.4(a) of DILG-DOE Circular No. 98-01 on the utilization of the Share from the National Wealth be included in plans and budgets of, and be implemented and charged against the 80% component of the Share from the National Wealth of the Municipality.

We recommended the following: a. The Municipal Accountant properly record inventories in the books using the perpetual inventory method and adjust their reporting of costs using the moving average method. Inventories recorded as expenses upon their release or issue to end-user departments or offices be reverted to their respective inventory accounts when, during the physical count, it was found out that they were unconsumed or undistributed. b. Prospectively, Chapter 6, Volume I of the GAM for LGUs be complied with in the recording, accounting, reporting and management of inventories.

We recommended the following: a. The accountable officers (AOs) of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office who were granted cash advances for emergency subsidy to households under the SAP, in coordination the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO), immediately and completely submit to the Municipal Accountant, the Reports of Disbursements (RDs) and their required supporting documents. b. Immediately submit the RDs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor once proper liquidation in the books had been recorded.

We recommended the following: a. The Local Chief Executive issue demand letters and cause the immediate liquidation of the long outstanding cash advances of officers and employees. Salaries and emoluments of accountable officers who are still employed with the Municipality be immediately withheld and the same be applied in satisfaction of their unliquidated cash advances. b. No additional cash advances to officers and employees be granted unless prior cash advances are completely and fully liquidated. Partial liquidation of cash advances is not allowed.

We recommended the following: a. The Municipal Accountant send demand letters to accountable officers, employees or recipients with long outstanding liabilities with the Municipality and exhaust all possible remedies to enforce the collection of the receivables. b. The Municipal Accountant analyze and reconcile all dormant receivable accounts, gather supporting documents, determine their condition and timely prepare reports related thereto. c. The Local Chief Executive file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to the Auditor when valid conditions exist following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Management ensure that all disbursements charged out of the 80% component` of the Share from the National Wealth of the Municipality be supported with documents showing that the provisions of Section 294 of RA No. 7160, Section 66 of RA No. 9136 and its IRR and Section 2.4(a) of DILG-DOE Circular No. 98-01 on the utilization of the Share from the National Wealth have been observed.

We recommended the following: a. The Municipal Administrator take over the duty of countersigning checks in settlement of the Municipality’s obligations from the Municipal Mayor. The next in rank second-level permanent staff/employee in the Office of the Municipal Administrator take over the countersigning of checks during the Municipal Administrator’s temporary absence or incapacity. b. The Municipal Treasurer assist in making representation with the Municipality’s depository banks that the Municipal Administrator, the Municipal Vice-Mayor and their next-in-ranks/OIC are the official signatories of the Municipality’s checks and in the taking of their specimen signatures.

We recommended that:
a. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01; b. Management provide budgetary and technical support to the MPDO in the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database; and c. The GAD Focal Point System – Technical Working Group use sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information provided by the GAD database in identifying gender issues and gaps and their related projects and activities for inclusion in the gender-responsive GPB.

We reiterated our recommendation that:
a. Submit to the Municipal Accountant as basis for derecognizing from the books the aforementioned PPEs to ensure the correctness of the PPE account reflected in the financial statements; and b. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize the properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Officials concerned should submit the above-mentioned documents under COA Circular Nos. 2009-01 and 2012-01 dated February 12, 2009 and July 14, 2012 respectively, so that technical review and evaluation can be immediately conducted.

We recommended that the OIC, Office of the Municipal Accountant and the In Charge of Office, Municipal Treasurer’s Office address the cause of delay in the submission of the disbursement vouchers and all of its pertinent documents to ensure submission within the prescribed period in compliance with COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

Disbursement vouchers totaling P50,189,167.75 in CY 2012 to CY 2015 were not submitted to the Auditor for post audit due to laxity in the proper maintenance of records and reports, absence of controls and safeguards in the custody and safekeeping of documents contrary to Section 39 (1) (3) of PD 1445, Section 6 of COA Circular 95-006 and Section 70 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System for LGU’s, Volume 1. Moreover, the interim reports were not prepared and submitted within the prescribed period, contrary to Section 73 of the same Manual.

We recommended that the OIC, Office of the Municipal Accountant and the In Charge of Office, Municipal Treasurer’s Office address the cause of delay in the submission of the disbursement vouchers and all of its pertinent documents to ensure submission within the prescribed period in compliance with COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

a. The Municipal Accountant, guided by COA Circular No. 2024-006,
prepare adjusting entries to implement the new accounting policy on
capitalization threshold and the recognition of semi-expendable
properties;
b. To facilitate the timely adjustment of accounts, the Municipal
Accountant identify the accumulated depreciation of each of the PPE
items that would be recognized as semi-expendable property for the
amounts are needed in the closing of the afore-said accounts;
c. The Acting Municipal General Services Officer, to assist in the timely
adjustment of accounts in the books to comply with the new
capitalization threshold, immediately effect changes and/or replacement
of property accountability forms such as e.g., Property
Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) to Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) and
Property Cards (PCs) to Semi-Expendable Property Cards (SPCs), and
furnish the Municipal Accountant copies thereof.
d. Prospectively, the Management comply with the guidelines set forth in
COA Circular No. 2022-004 as supplemented by COA Circular No.
2024-006 in implementing the new accounting policy on capitalization
threshold and the recognition of semi-expendable properties including
the use of the new property accountability forms.

Treasurer, on or before December 31 of each year, the assessment roll
containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly
assessed or reassessed, along with the corresponding assessed values of
such properties;
b. The Municipal Treasurer then update the Real Property Tax Account
Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card based on the latest/updated
assessment roll provided by the Municipal Assessor and provide the
Municipal Accountant the computation of tax collectibles for the year;
c. The Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to
record the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based
on the information provided by the Municipal Treasurer on the amount
due and collectible for the year, using the following pro-forma entry;
and
Account Debit Credit
Real Property Tax Receivable xxx
Special Education Tax Receivable xxx
Real Property Tax - Basic xxx
Special Education Tax xxx
To record the set-up of receivables at the beginning of the year.
d. The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments in the
books to correct the reported balances of RPT and SET Receivables as
at reporting period.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer,
in coordination with the Municipal Accountant for accounting/recording
purposes, transfer all receipts pertaining to the SNW to the separate
depository bank account created for the purpose as a good control
mechanism and to minimize the risk that cash pertaining to SNW may be
utilized for other purposes that what it is specifically intended to, i.e., for
local development and livelihood projects or lowering the cost of
electricity, as the case may be.

a. The Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and the Human Resource Management
Officer-Designate assist the Local Chief Executive in the
rationalization of the jobs that need to be carried out in the
implementation of the different programs of the Municipality and the
rightsizing in the hiring of job order personnel to avoid mismatch in the
assignment of work of such personnel and the mischarging of their
wages; and
b. The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, when
certifying as to the availability of funds for the purpose and the
completeness of supporting documents, respectively, during payments
of wages of job order personnel, see to it that the jobs and work
accomplishments these personnel perform coincide with and are
supportive of the objectives of the office/program from where their
wages are charged.

a. The Municipal Treasurer, through the Municipal Accountant,
immediately submit all the lacking check disbursement vouchers with
complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for audit;
and
b. Specifically, the Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the Officer-In-
Charge of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to
immediately return the signed vouchers of financial assistances, release
of checks of which their staff facilitated, for immediate submission to
the Office of the Auditor.

a. The Municipal Treasurer transfer all the unexpended/unobligated
balances of the QRF and DRRMF – MOOE from CY 2020 to CY 2024
to the DBP Account No. 00684-705-3 for the effective management of
the fund;
b. The Municipal Accountant then record the transfer from the General
Fund to Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities –
DRRMF, using the following pro-forma entries;
Account Debit Credit
Under General Fund
Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds
to the Trust Funds (Current Year) xxx
Prior Period Adjustments (Prior Years) xxx
Cash in Bank xxx
To record the transfer of unexpended LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund.
Under Trust Fund
Cash in Bank xxx
Trust Liabilities – DRRMF xxx
To record the receipt of the unexpended LDRRMF from the General Fund.
c. Prospectively, the Municipal Treasurer transfer all the
unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF, DRRMF – MOOE and

accounts; and
d. The Municipal Treasurer transfer the unutilized amount of LDRRMF
after five years, which is from CY 2016 to CY 2019, back to the
unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for subsequent
enactment.

a. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and
maintain a GAD database which should contain complete data
requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2024-01;
b. Management provide budgetary and technical support to the MPDO
in the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database; and
c. The GAD Focal Person/Focal Point System – Technical Working
Group use sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information
provided by the GAD database in identifying gender issues and gaps
and their related projects and activities for inclusion in the gender-
responsive GPB.

a. The GAD Focal Person, with the assistance of the Municipal Budget
Officer, see to it that the HGDG tool is administered to all PAPs being
attributed to the GAD budget and the results of the HGDG assessment
together with the supporting documents be attached to the GPB; and
b. Management ensure that necessary training on HGDG be provided to
key personnel who prepare/consolidate the Municipality’s yearly GPB
so that the required minimum 5% GAD budget is computed correctly
and accurately.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management enforce the settlement of
the disallowances in compliance to COA Circular No. 2009-006 and COA
Memorandum No. 2009-084.

We recommended and Management agreed that, prospectively:
a. The Municipal Accountant record all undistributed goods/inventories purchased out of transferred funds and their corresponding inter-agency liability in the Trust Fund books. Only when such goods are distributed and fund utilization reports are submitted to the source agency shall the inventories be recognized as expenses and the liability diminished.
b. Management hasten the immediate distribution of the purchased goods/inventories to facilitate timely liquidation of the funds with the source agency and so that benefits from the project may already accrue to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended and Management agreed that Management regulate mobile communication and internet usage and expenditures of elective officials and department heads ensuring that the amount of mobile communication allowance and internet subscriptions allowance per official/head is not excessive, reasonable, duly justified and with reference to the current price of such services in the market.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following: a. The Bids and Awards Committee conduct meetings only within the locality or in venues accessible to stakeholders and the public to ensure transparency and economy of the procurement process. b. The Municipal Accountant ensure that all claims for travel is supported with written authorities to travel specific for every activity and back-to-office reports, if necessary. c. The Human Resource Management Officer IV rationalize the conduct of TBA as part of the Municipality’s staff development activity and see to it that its conduct follows established rules and guidelines.

We recommended and Management agreed that: a. The Bids and Awards Committee strictly follow the guidelines and support their recommendation for award for opportunities under Negotiated Procurement – Scientific, Scholarly or Artistic Work, Exclusive Technology and Media Services with the documents required in Annex “H” the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. b. The Municipal Accountant see to it that all payments for Scientific, Scholarly or Artistic Work, Exclusive Technology and Media Services are supported with complete documents and the necessary taxes pertaining thereto are withheld. c. The Municipal Treasurer ensure that only the allowed types of transactions enumerated in COA Circular No. 97-002 are to be paid through the cash advances of disbursing officers.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant facilitate the submission of the cash advance voucher for CF and all its supporting documents to the COA ATL within seven (7) days after release of check for timely evaluation and audit.
b. Cash advances by the SDO for CF be made quarterly and limited only to requirements for three months following the timelines provided in the Physical and Financial Plan.

We recommended and Management agreed that: a. The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the DOH to clarify the expiration date of the HFEP funds received to implement the project “Completion/ Upgrading/Repair of Hininga-an Barangay Health Station”. If the funds are still unexpired, expedite to resolve the issues so the project will be implemented already within the year. Otherwise, if the funds were already expired as at December 31, 2023, the Municipal Treasurer immediately return the unutilized fund balance to the national treasury for it is no longer valid and available for use. b. Prospectively, Management see to it that projects to be implemented from grants, donations and assistance of private and other government organizations which have expirations periods or are time-bounded should be prioritized and implemented timely and in accordance with the agreed terms and conditions and/or guidelines to avoid reversion of the funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain a Cashbook for Cash in Bank for all funds and bank accounts in the required form pursuant to the requirements and instructions provided in the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.

We recommended and Management agreed that: a. The Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the concerned barangays for the immediate submission of FURs, showing the unutilized balance and the amount to be refunded, if any, pertaining to the fund transfers. b. The submitted FURs be made basis by the Municipal Accountant in the recording of the liquidation of the funds transfers in the Municipality’s books. c. As a control measure, Management ensure that no additional financial assistance be granted to barangays unless prior fund transfers were completely and fully liquidated. Partial liquidation of fund transfer shall not be allowed.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following: a. The Municipal Accountant prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate its timely submission. b. The LDRRMO then submit the report to the Office of the Auditor on or before 15th day after the end of each month for us to review and/or evaluate the fund sources, fund allocations in each thematic area and the expenditures charged against the fund.

We recommended that Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in its activities to improve the efficiency of collection of real property taxes by including in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the said activities and providing augmentation of personnel when needed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, strictly observe that appropriations for and expenditures for EME of the Local Chief Executive comply with the limit set in Section 325(h) of RA No. 7160.
We recommended that the Management, in its future disbursement of funds, adhere to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and other related issuances.

We recommended the following: a. The Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the concerned barangays for the immediate submission of RCIs, RDs and Utilization Reports, showing the unutilized balance and the amount to be refunded, if any, pertaining to the fund transfers. b. The Municipal Engineer conduct technical inspections and validate the reported status of the projects implemented by the barangays out of the transferred funds. c. The submitted RCIs, RDs and Utilization Reports, and the inspection reports of the Municipal Engineer be made bases by the Municipal Accountant in the recording of the liquidation of the fund transfers in the Municipality’s books.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Accountant:

a.	Record all valid reconciling items appearing in the BRSs that require adjustments and/or corrections in the books.



b.	Secure supporting documents of the unrecorded check disbursements totaling ₱16.978 million so they may likewise be dropped as reconciling items in the BRS and properly recorded in the books.


c.	Cancel stale checks and prepare adjusting/correcting entries to restore the amounts to the Cash in Bank account. For checks which became stale in the hands of the payee that require replacement, issue a new check upon submission of the stale check and other necessary documents and prepare the appropriate accounting entries.

The submitted RCIs, RDs and Utilization Reports, and the inspection reports of the Municipal Engineer be made bases by the Municipal Accountant in the recording of the liquidation of the fund transfers in the Municipality’s books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant send demand letters to accountable officers, employees or recipients with long outstanding liabilities with the Municipality and exhaust all possible remedies to enforce the collection of the receivables.

The Municipal Accountant analyze and reconcile all dormant receivable accounts, gather supporting documents, determine their condition and timely prepare reports related thereto.

The Local Chief Executive file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to the Auditor when valid conditions exist following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason:
No demand letters were issued during the year.



Not implemented
No reconciliation of other receivable accounts was made during the period.


Not implemented
The Agency is on the process of requesting for write-off the Other Receivables account and all other dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure that all disbursements charged out of the 80% component` of the Share from the National Wealth of the Municipality be supported with documents showing that the provisions of Section 294 of RA No. 7160, Section 66 of RA No. 9136 and its IRR and Section 2.4(a) of DILG-DOE Circular No. 98-01 on the utilization of the Share from the National Wealth have been observed.
observations.reason: We observed that several projects in CY 2023 were still paid out of the 80% SNW despite that none or only a portion of their works is attributable to lowering the cost of electricity. Nevertheless, there is a pending court petition for declaratory relief filed by the Municipality before the Regional Trial Court (RTC) Branch 13, Culasi, Antique and a pending query for a legal opinion filed before the Department of Energy (DOE).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The Municipal Administrator take over the duty of countersigning checks in settlement of the Municipality’s obligations from the Municipal Mayor. The next in rank second-level permanent staff/employee in the Office of the Municipal Administrator take over the countersigning of checks during the Municipal Administrator’s temporary absence or incapacity.

The Municipal Treasurer assist in making representation with the Municipality’s depository banks that the Municipal Administrator, the Municipal Vice-Mayor and their next-in-ranks/OIC are the official signatories of the Municipality’s checks and in the taking of their specimen signatures.
observations.reason:
Checks issued out of the executive appropriations were still signed by the Municipal Mayor up to date.



Not implemented
No representation was made with the Municipality’s depository banks as at date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. 

Management provide budgetary and technical support to the MPDO in the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database.

The GAD Focal Point System – Technical Working Group use sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information provided by the GAD database in identifying gender issues and gaps and their related projects and activities for inclusion in the gender-responsive GPB.
observations.reason:
GAD database was not yet created and maintained despite that statistical information from the CBMS is already available to be incorporated and utilized.

Not implemented
Efforts were made on creation of GAD database, but it was not carried out up to date.

Since there is an absence of database, we could not analyze the information that should be contained therein.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 	We reiterated and Management agreed with the following recommendations:
The Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to record major repairs and replacements in CY 2023 and prior years to the carrying amounts of items of PPE with provisions for depreciation and/or impairment using the following pro-forma entry:
Account					Debit		Credit
Property, Plant and Equipment			        xxx		                   Prior Period Adjustments			                 xxx
Accumulated Depreciation					                 xxx
Accumulated Impairment Losses			                 xxx
To record major repairs and replacements in CY 2023 and prior years to the carrying amounts of items of PPE with provisions for depreciation and impairment.
Likewise, the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to record the derecognition of replaced PPE using the following pro-forma entry:
Account					Debit		Credit
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory	                             xxx
Accumulated Depreciation 	                xxx
Property, Plant and Equipment				     xxx
To record derecognition of replaced PPE and recognize its scrap value.
The Municipal Budget Officer, in the preparation of future executive budgets, see to it that budgets for major repairs and replacements of PPE, and in case of procured spare parts amounting to ₱50,000.00 or more per unit, be charged out of the capital outlay appropriations and not under the MOOE appropriations. Budgetary accounts be unified and harmonized with the accounts used in the recording of financial transactions in the books.
Prospectively, the Municipal Accountant record major repairs and replacements of parts of PPE in accordance with the recognition principles provided in IPSAS 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment.

2.7	We recommended and Management agreed that, prospectively:
The Municipal Accountant record all undistributed goods/inventories purchased out of the transferred funds and their corresponding inter-agency liability in the Trust Fund books. Only when such goods are distributed and fund utilization reports are submitted to the source agency shall the inventories be recognized as expenses and the liability diminished.
Management hasten the immediate distribution of the purchased goods/inventories to facilitate timely liquidation of the funds with the source agency and so that benefits from the project may already accrue to the intended beneficiaries.

3.7		We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant for accounting/recording purposes, transfer all receipts pertaining to the 80% SNW to the separate depository bank account created for the purpose as a good control mechanism and to minimize the risk that cash pertaining to 80% SNW may be utilized for other purposes that what it is specifically intended to, i.e., for programs and projects related to the lowering of the cost of electricity in the locality.

4.6	We recommended that Management regulate mobile communication and internet usage and expenditures of elective officials and department heads ensuring that the amount of mobile communication allowance and internet subscriptions allowance per official/head is not excessive, reasonable, duly justified and with reference to the current price of such services in the market.

5.12 	We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
The Bids and Awards Committee conduct meetings only within the locality or in venues accessible to stakeholders and the public to ensure transparency and economy of the procurement process.
The Municipal Accountant ensure that all claims for travel is supported with written authorities to travel specific for every activity and back-to-office reports, if necessary.
The Human Resource Management Officer IV rationalize the conduct of TBA as part of the Municipality’s staff development activity and see to it that its conduct follows established rules and guidelines.

6.7	We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Bids and Awards Committee strictly follow the guidelines and support their recommendation for award for opportunities under Negotiated Procurement – Scientific, Scholarly or Artistic Work, Exclusive Technology and Media Services with the documents required in Annex “H” the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
The Municipal Accountant see to it that payments for Scientific, Scholarly or Artistic Work, Exclusive Technology and Media Services are supported with complete documents and the necessary taxes pertaining thereto are withheld.
The Municipal Treasurer ensure that only the allowed types of transactions enumerated in COA Circular No. 97-002 are to be paid through the cash advances of disbursing officers.

7.9	We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant facilitate the submission of the cash advance voucher for CF and all its supporting documents to the COA ATL within seven (7) days after release of check for timely evaluation and audit.
Cash advances by the SDO for CF be made quarterly and limited only to requirements for three months following the timelines provided in the Physical and Financial Plan.

8.7	We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the DOH to clarify the expiration date of the HFEP funds received to implement the project “Completion/ Upgrading/Repair of Hininga-an Barangay Health Station”. If the funds are still unexpired, expedite to resolve the issues so the project will be implemented already within the year. Otherwise, if the funds were already expired as at December 31, 2023, the Municipal Treasurer immediately return the unutilized fund balance to the national treasury for it is no longer valid and available for use.
Prospectively, Management see to it that projects to be implemented from grants, donations and assistance of private and other government organizations which have expirations periods or are time-bounded should be prioritized and implemented timely and in accordance with the agreed terms and conditions and/or guidelines to avoid reversion of the funds.

9.5  	We recommended the following and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain a Cashbook for Cash in Bank for all funds and bank accounts in the required form pursuant to the requirements and instructions provided in the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.

10.6 	We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the concerned barangays for the immediate submission of FURs, showing the unutilized balance and the amount to be refunded, if any, pertaining to the fund transfers.
The submitted FURs be made basis by the Municipal Accountant in the recording of the liquidation of fund transfers in the Municipality’s books.
As a control measure, Management ensure that no additional financial assistance be granted to barangays unless prior fund transfers were completely and fully liquidated. Partial liquidation of fund transfer shall not be allowed.

11.5 	We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
The Municipal Accountant prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate its timely submission.
The LDRRMO then submit the report to the Office of the Auditor on or before 15th day after the end of each month for us to review and/or evaluate the fund sources, fund allocations in each thematic area and the expenditures charged against the fund.
We reiterated our recommendation that Management enforce the settlement of the disallowances in compliance to COA Circular No. 2009-006 and COA Memorandum No. 2009-084.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant include in the Notes to FSs the required disclosures on the reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the SCBAA and in the SFPerf for the year then ended. Its format be based on Annex G of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We recommended the following:

a.	The Municipal Accountant immediately submit all the lacking 381 check disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for audit.

b.	The Municipal Treasurer hasten the release of unclaimed checks so that the related check disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents may be forwarded to the Municipal Accountant who shall submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended the following:

a.	The BAC Chairperson monitor that all purchases of the end-users of the Municipality are coursed through their office and procured in accordance with RA No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR (Updated as of March 31, 2021).


b.	Management discontinue the practice of purchasing goods and services directly from suppliers and paying them through the cash advances of special disbursing officers.

We recommended that in order to ensure that funds transferred to NGOs/POs are solely utilized for public purposes:

a.	Management make certain that fund transfers to NGOs/POs comply with the guidelines set in COA Circular No. 2007-001.

b.	The Municipal Accountant see to it that all supporting documents as enumerated in the afore-said Circular are attached before funds are released to NGOs/POs and the release are properly accounted and recorded in the books to enforce the submission of FURs.

We recommended that the municipal department heads see to it that information media like posters, streamers, tarpaulins and signages covering the implementation of programs and projects under their offices do not contain or are associated with any municipal official’s names, initials and/or images.

We recommended that:

a.	The Municipal Mayor utilize the CF from which confidential expenses are to be charged following the guidelines set in COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND JC No. 2015-01.

b.	Refrain from paying confidential peace and order expenses through reimbursement to the Chief of Police. Expenditures for regular peace and order program be coursed through the usual procurement process as provided in Republic Act No. 9184 and its revised IRR.

We again commended and congratulated the Municipality and its management for having received awards in CY 2022. We recommended that the Municipality’s officials and employees continuously improve and sustain these local good governance efforts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an accounting entry to adjust the recording of the RDT kits from Inventories to Adjustment in Net Assets/Equity.

We recommended that the Management ensure that all donations in kind received by the Municipality during the quarantine period due to the COVID-19 pandemic be reported through a Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and submitted within the required reglementary period to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), through the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD), copy furnished the COA Auditors.

We recommended that the Municipality’s obligations be paid by check under the general principles of the imprest system and refrain from payment in cash out of the cash advance in instances other than those prescribed under COA Circular 97 – 002.
We recommended that Management adhere with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012 – 001 on the liquidation of cash advance and CSC MC No. 17 s. 2002 as to the rates of job order personnel.

We reiterated the submission of the required documents enumerated under Section 4.4 of the Circular to establish the legal entity and financial capability of the NGO/PO.

Review and evaluate the terms and conditions of the second MOA executed on January 19, 2016 and ensure the collection of the remaining loan amounting to ₱8,744.000.00 to the advantage of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Compute for the amortization of all intangible assets;
Make necessary adjustments in the books based on the computation; and
Provide yearly amortization for all intangible assets in accordance with the above-mentioned standards.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Accountant:
Compute for the amortization of all intangible assets;
Make necessary adjustments in the books based on the computation; and
Provide yearly amortization for all intangible assets in accordance with the above-mentioned standards.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management review the proper recording and reporting of biological assets in the books of accounts and financial statements of the Municipality. Specifically:
The Property Officer submit to the Municipal Accountant the necessary supporting documents as basis for derecognizing from the books non-existent biological assets to ensure the correctness of presentation of the Biological Assets account in the financial statements; and
The Municipal Accountant prepare journal entry to derecognize the above-mentioned biological assets when supporting documents are already in order.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant include in the Notes to FSs the required disclosures on the reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the SCBAA and in the SFPerf for the year then ended. Its format be based on Annex G of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We recommended the following:
a. 	The Sangguniang Bayan, through a resolution, rationalize and update the rules and regulations of the Municipality on the grant of scholarship, incorporating corrections of deficiencies noted in audit such as submission of other proofs of income/indigency/case studies, consideration of a reasonable ceiling in the amount of tuition fee subsidy in the average grade requirement/bracketing system, revisiting the current exemptions to make the grants more fair, just and equitable to all, and strict imposition and monitoring of the return service clause to include penal provision of return/refund in case of non-fulfilment of the scholar;
b. 	The Municipal Mayor implement strictly and with no exemptions the provisions of the rules and regulations of the Municipality on the grant of scholarship once revised and updated; and
c. 	The Municipal Accountant see to it that all supporting documents including additional proofs of income/indigency/case studies are attached to the disbursement vouchers covering the payments of scholarship.

We recommended the following:
The SB, through a resolution, establish well-defined guidelines on the grant of AICS by setting maximum amount for each type of financial assistance, provide for frequency of availment and require the submission of a certification that the beneficiary is under crisis situation.
The Municipal Mayor and the MSWDO implement strictly the guidelines on the AICS Program once revised and updated.
The Municipal Accountant ensure that documents attached to all claims under the AICS Program are complete including the case studies and the necessary certifications of indigency and in crisis situation.

We recommended that the members of the Local Finance Committee, the Municipal Energization Head and the Municipal Accountant see to it that only projects or their work components that may qualify to the requirements set by the provisions of Section 294 of RA No. 7160, Section 66 of RA No. 9136 and its IRR and Section 2.4(a) of DILG-DOE Circular No. 98-01 on the utilization of the Share from the National Wealth be included in plans and budgets of, and be implemented and charged against the 80% component of the Share from the National Wealth of the Municipality.

We recommended that the BAC provide complete documentation and strictly comply with the requirements of Annex “G” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the procurement and implementation of infrastructure contracts under the Design and Build scheme.

We recommended that Management:
Only hire COS contractors if there are special projects that are to be accomplished in a specified period of time or the jobs are highly technical and the required expertise is not available in the Municipality, and they be paid only upon accomplishment of deliverables, written reports or outputs required in the required TOR; and
Hasten the hiring of a Municipal Legal Officer or avail of, for the meantime, the services of the Provincial Legal Officer or the Provincial Prosecutor with regard to the legal needs of the Municipality. Hiring of COS contractors to provide legal services is strictly prohibited by RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 98-002.

We recommended the following:
The HRMO, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, prepare the monthly payroll for salaries to include only names of personnel who have completely submitted their DTRs and their supporting documents. Concerned employees who had not initially submitted their DTRs on time be paid of their salaries only upon submission thereof, and penalties, surcharges and interests due as a result of delayed withholding and remittance of taxes, contributions or loans shall be personally borne by them;
The Municipal Administrator and the HRMO institute disciplinary action against personnel who habitually fail to submit their DTRs on time in accordance with civil service rules and regulations; and
The Municipal Mayor instruct each of the Department Heads to strictly monitor the submission of DTRs of employees under their respective offices and to consolidate and summarize their attendance and absences by preparing the Monthly Report of Attendance, Absences and Undertimes for submission to the HRMO.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor or the Municipal Vice-Mayor coordinate with the DBM and the CSC regarding the legality and validity of the appointments in question. As the appointing authority, they shall be personally liable for the payment of salaries of personnel when their appointments are invalidated by the CSC on grounds that constitute a violation of pertinent laws, rules and regulation in accordance with the CSC’s Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointments and Other Human Resource Actions; and
The Municipal Budget Officer, together with the Human Resource Management Officer, propose for a reorganization and redesign of the personnel complement structure and staffing pattern of the Municipality which shall be also reflected in its Annual Budget. Department Head and Assistant Department Head positions shall be created/appropriated/appointed only when the office they head is composed of at least four divisions pursuant to CSC MC No. 19 s. 1992. Also, positions and salary grades of the immediate support staff of the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Vice-Mayor shall be limited in accordance with DBM CL No. 2007-6.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Engineer re-assume the duties and responsibilities of the Local Building Official pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991. Appropriations for and expenditures pertaining to the Office of the Local Building Official, including the necessary support for its operations, be lodged under the Office of the Municipal Engineer; and
The Human Resource and Management Officer and the Municipal Budget Officer coordinate with the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan to consider recalling their issuances for being ultra vires and contrary to law, and facilitate that the affected assistant municipal department head re-assume his previous designation.

We commended and congratulated the Municipality and its management for having received the awards. We recommended that the Municipality’s officials and employees continuously improve and sustain these local good governance efforts.
We reiterated our recommendation that Management enforce the settlement of the disallowances in compliance to COA Circular No. 2009-006 and COA Memorandum No. 2009-084.

a. The Municipal Accountant record maintenance and other operating expenditures to their appropriate accounts considering the nature or purpose of the transactions, and prospectively, in consonance with what is prescribed in the Chart of Accounts embodied in Volume III of the GAM for LGUs. Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account be used only to record expenses not falling under any specific maintenance and other operating expense accounts.
b. The Municipal Budget Officer, in the preparation of future executive budgets, see to it that special purpose appropriations and all other programs, projects and activities are not listed in lump-sum, in single line-item, or only chargeable against the Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account, but instead be provided with the appropriate accounts. Budgetary accounts be unified and harmonized with the accounts used in the recording of financial transactions in the books.

Treasurer exercise due

We recommended that the unexpended balance of the share in the national wealth which the municipality considered as surplus be properly disclosed as appropriated surplus, being not available for appropriation except those for the specific purpose for which it was intended.  It may also be accounted as Trust Liability since the fund is not available for operational appropriation in the succeeding years.  We also recommended proper accounting of the previous balances and monitoring of its usage to ensure compliance with the applicable provisions of the local government code on the utilization of the share in national wealth.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prioritize the preparation of the updated Bank Reconciliation Statements for all its depository bank accounts to ensure correctness of the cash in bank balance and  furnish the Auditor a copy thereof.

We recommend that cash advances for payrolls shall be equal to the net amount per duly approved payrolls. Require as supporting document, a list of payrolls, detailing the nature of expenses and the total amounts due to be paid. Unliquidated balances after payrolls are paid shall be returned or refunded to the local treasury. All grant and liquidation of cash advances shall comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer instruct all  collectors and tellers to properly accomplish Section  C of the Report  of  Collections  and  Deposits to facilitate the preparation of the individual Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms as an input to the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms of the Office during the month.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive convene the Committee created or in-charge for the appraisal of the unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 170 of Republic Act No. 7160 so that the Municipal General Services Officer could dispose the unserviceable property of the Local Government in accordance with Sections 167, 169 and 170 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

We reiterated our recommendation that:


     1. The Inventory Committee complete its inventory taking  on  all  LGU
        property and prepare the  complete  RPCPPE  after  the  conduct  of
        physical count and furnish a copy to the Office of the Auditor.


     2. The Property Officer reconcile property cards maintained  with  the
        RPCPPE and discrepancies, if any be verified and adjusted.


     3. The Municipal Accountant likewise reconcile PPE  ledger  cards  and
        other accounting records with the property cards and the RPCPPE.


     4. The Municipal Accountant provide and  record  the  depreciation  of
        depreciable PPE in accordance with the guidelines set  by  relevant
        COA regulations.

We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant despite  geographical
       constraints prepare  and  submit  promptly  and  regularly  the  bank
      reconciliation statements to the Office of the Auditor  as  prescribed
      by COA Circular 96 – 011.  To ensure accuracy of the Cash  in  Bank  –
      LCCA we also recommended the following:

        1.  The  Municipal  Treasurer  and  the  OIC  Municipal  Accountant
           reconcile the balance of the Cash in  Bank  LCCA  per  books  of
           accounts and Municipal Treasurer’s cashbook at least quarterly.


        2. The Municipal Accountant record all valid reconciling items that
           require adjustments  and  corrections  in  the  books.  Identify
           checks which became  stale  and  prepare  adjusting  entries  to
           revert the amounts to the Cash in Bank account.


        3. Make representation regarding items that  need  adjustments  and
           corrections by the depository bank.


        4. BRS submitted to the Auditor  be  supported  with  Monthly  Bank
           Statements and complete documents in support to all  reconciling
           items.

We recommended that the OIC, Office of the  Municipal  Accountant  and
      the In Charge of Office,  Municipal  Treasurer’s  Office  address  the
      cause of delay in the submission of the disbursement vouchers and  all
      of its pertinent supporting documents to ensure submission within  the
      prescribed period in compliance with COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May
      18, 1995.

We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  and  the  Municipal
      Accountant  strictly  observe  the   guidelines   in   the   granting,
      utilization and liquidation of cash advances in  accordance  with  COA
      Circular 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012 – 04 as follows:


              1. Cash advance for special purpose and time bound  activities
                 be liquidated within twenty (20) days  from  accomplishment
                 of the purpose;


              2. Cash advance for Salaries, Wages, Allowances, Honoraria and
                 Other Similar Payments shall be equal to the net amount  of
                 the payroll for a pay period;


              3. Properly monitor the liquidation of the cash advances;


              4. Require that no additional cash advance be  granted  unless
                 previous cash advances have been fully liquidated;


              5. Demand  immediate  liquidation  of  the  unliquidated  cash
                 advances; and

              6. Withhold the salary of official  or  employee  in  case  of
                 failure to liquidate  cash  advances  and  institute  legal
                 sanctions in accordance with laws if warranted.

We recommended that  the  Municipality  of  Caluya  comply  with  the
      provisions of RA 9184  and  the  IRR  in  the  hiring  of  individual
      consultants:


          1. The  BAC  observe  the  procedures  required  by  the  Generic
             Procurement Manual for Local Government Units on the hiring of
             individual consultants under Section 53.7 of  the  IRR  of  RA
             9184.


          2.  The BAC   undertake  negotiation  with,  and  validate   the
             capability of the  individual  consultants  to  undertake  and
             fulfill the consultancy work based on the Terms of  Reference,
             and to recommend to the  Municipal  Mayor  the  award  of  the
             contract.


          3. In accordance with Section 54.6(e) of the 2016 Revised IRR  of
             RA No. 9184, individual consultants are mandated  to  register
             with PhilGEPS and provide a PhilGEPS registration number as  a
             condition for award of contract, thus the  BAC  ensure  strict
             compliance with this requirement.


      We  also  recommended  that  the  LGU  discontinue  the  payment   of
      reimbursements of the actual travelling expenses of the consultants.

We recommended that Purchase Orders be provided with complete
      information and details in accordance with COA Circular No. 96  –  010
      dated August 15, 1996.   We  further  recommended  compliance  of  the
      provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of goods.

We recommended the following:


        1. The OIC Municipal Accountant, Planning Officer II  and  the  BAC
           Chairperson  submit  the  complete   supporting   documents   in
           prescribed format in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012 –  01
           dated June 14, 2012 and RA 7160.


        2. Strict compliance to the  posting  of  the  Invitation  to  Bid,
           Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and contracts in the PhilGEPS
           website in accordance  with  the  pertinent  provisions  of  the
           Revised IRR of RA 9184.

        3. Strictly observe the period of action on procurement  activities
           in accordance with the IRR of RA 9184.

        4. Contracts and its integral part be submitted within  the  period
           prescribed under COA Circular 2009-01.

        5. Proper authorization be  secured  first  before  dispensing  any
           delegated authority in accordance with the IRR of RA 9184 and RA
           7160,  specifically  in  the  procurement   process   and   such
           authorization shall form part of the supporting document for the
           payments.

We  recommended   that   the   Municipal   Accountant   transfer   the
      unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF presently recorded in the
      General Fund to a Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund Books in  order
      to ensure continued and exclusive use of the  fund  for  disaster-risk
      reduction management  activities  within  the  next  five  years.  The
      accounting entries are shown in COA Circular No. 2012-002  for  proper
      guidance.

We recommended that the LCE in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016 –
      004 dated September 30, 2016 require that:


        1. Appropriation be provided for expenses such that the requirement
           on proper classification and  use  of  the  appropriate  account
           title and codes consistent with the prescribed Chart of Accounts
           are properly observed to avoid mischarging of other expenses  to
           Other MOE.


        2. The Municipal Accountant  record  the  PPE,  inventory  and  the
           expenses in their appropriate account classification pursuant to
           the prescribed Chart of Accounts.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Planning  and  Development  Coordinator
properly plan the programs/projects funded under the  20%  development  fund
and ensure its implementation during the year in accordance  with  DILG  and
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the timely submission of accounts and other financial reports. The Municipal Treasurer was strictly enjoined to issue checks in proper numerical order to facilitate monitoring and control and was instructed to submit all records of receipts and disbursements together with the supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant within the reglamentary period prescribed in the existing rules and regulations for timely preparation of financial statements and the submission of accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor. Strict adherence to the provisions of the above mentioned regulations was enjoined.

We recommended strict compliance with the Executive Orders issued by the Municipal Mayor on the conduct of physical inventory taking and the preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of Property and Equipment in its prescribed format and its ultimate reconciliation with the accounting records to support the correctness of the PPE accounts in the financial statements, otherwise accuracy of the amount of properties and equipment in the financial statements remains doubtful.

We recommended that documents in the procurement process of gasoline, fuel and oil be attached to the disbursement voucher to attest compliance with procurement laws, rules and regulations.  We also strongly recommended the issuance of Requisition and Issue Slip to document the issuance of supplies and materials to the person accountable in operating and maintaining the power house.  A Logbook was encouraged to be maintained by each generator as monitoring tool in the receipt and usage of crude oil.  It would also serve as source document in preparing the Operations Report showing the generator number/brand name, number of liters of crude oil received from supplier or Supply Office used and the number of days and hours of operation.  The information supports expenses of crude oil/gasoline consumed during a particular period.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer observe the procedural guidelines in the flow of cash collections from collectors, Liquidating Officer, if any and the Municipal Treasurer.  Deposits with the authorized depository bank be made by the Municipal Treasurer.  Ensure strict compliance to the timely preparation of Report of Collections and Deposits and remittance of daily collections by the collectors.  Also, closely monitor the preparation of the abstract of collections as basis of recording income and other receipts.  The Assistant Municipal Treasurer as In-Charge of Office, was required to reconcile his accountabilities with the accounting records and settle his outstanding balances immediately.  We also recommended review of adjusting entries made to properly account income/prior year’s adjustment and liabilities to other fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the documents enumerated under Section 4.4 of the Circular to establish the legal entity and financial capability of the NGO/PO. Since a total of P13,244,000.00 were already extended, close supervision of the operations and financial stability of the cooperative be observed. Demand installment payments as stipulated in the Memoranda of Agreement to recover the funds.

We recommended correcting and adjusting entries be made to correct the deficiencies in the recording of real property tax income, both in the general and special education fund.  Liabilities to Special Education Fund and to other local government units be recognized in the general fund.  Also a receivable from the general fund be recorded in the special education fund, liabilities to other local government units and adjustments in income accounts.   We also recommend the review of prior years’ entries to ensure correctness of the accounts involved.  The Real Property Tax Receivable in both funds and the corresponding deferred account should be equal.

We recommended that the Box A of the Obligation Requests supporting the disbursement vouchers for the utilization of the Special Education Fund be approved/certified by the District Supervisor as co-chairperson of the Local School Board.

We recommended that the Assistant Municipal Treasurer, ICO strictly observe the procedural steps in issuing and reporting of checks issued.  Checks should be issued chronologically and reservations should not be tolerated.  Report of Checks Issued should be prepared monthly summarizing, in chronological order, checks issued and released to payees.  A separate Report of Unreleased Checks be prepared to account for checks not released to payees at the end of the month.  We also strongly recommended the regular preparation of bank reconciliation statements to reconcile the balance of the cash in bank per bank records and the books.

We recommended that a comparison of the results of the inspection per item of work be made against the Contractor’s Accomplishment Report to determine whether there was under or overpayment to the contractor.  Liquidated damages be imposed on the contractor, if warranted.  The municipality’s Inspectorate Team consider the condition of the structures, its strength and durability and assess if continuation of the project is still feasible.  If the municipality opts to continue the project based on original plan, the terrain, slope and site situation must be considered.  Otherwise, revise the plan to suit actual site conditions.

We recommended that the Accounting Office review its records for trust liabilities of prior years and establish corresponding accountabilities from employees with over remittances. Remittance list should be prepared based on employee’s payroll deductions, including those whose benefits were paid thru vouchers. Also, periodic review be made to ensure that what was withheld from the employee’s salaries or other benefits are remitted regularly.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance was liquidated. Demand the immediate refund of cash advance drawn whose trips were cancelled or cut short.  The Municipal Accountant also adopt measures to monitor and ensure the immediate liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that reimbursement of all medical, laboratory and hospital expenses of residents be discontinued.  Assistance to individuals in crisis situation maybe made in accordance with the regulations set by the Sangguniang Bayan.  Moreover, identification of indigent beneficiaries must strictly adhere to the requirements of who are considered as indigents or in crisis situation who can avail of the assistance that maybe granted by the local government unit.

We recommended that the Office of the Auditor be furnished copies of the documents enumerated above and all other forms of contracts and all its supporting documents to afford an effective and efficient review and evaluation of the reasonableness of prices and determination of the fairness and legality of the terms of agreement between contracting parties.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council observe the guidelines in the identification, appropriation and utilization of the 20% IRA for development projects required in Joint Circular 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG.  We also recommended avoidance of duplication of programs to facilitate monitoring and verification of accomplishments.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant observe the timely submission of accounts and other financial reports. The Municipal Treasurer is strictly enjoined to issue checks in its proper numerical order to facilitate monitoring and control and is instructed to submit all records of receipts and disbursements together with the supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant within the reglamentary period prescribed in the existing rules and regulations for timely preparation of financial statements and the submission of accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor. Strict adherence to the provisions of the above mentioned regulation is enjoined.

We recommend that collections be deposited directly to the bank within  the reglementary period or as often as transactions are made in the mainland where the depository banks are located.  We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer exercise due diligence in monitoring the collections and the preparation of the Abstract of Collections, remittances and deposits of collections to safeguard cash resources and ensure compliance with laws, rules and regulations.

We recommend that projects funded from 20% development be specific and pre-identified in accordance with its development plans, meeting the kind and classification as enumerated in Joint Circular No. 2011-1.  The preparation of program of work would assist in the allocation of funds to ensure its completion.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the persons involve in the implementation of the projects to facilitate  compliance with the observations of the Auditor as communicated and cause its early submission for verification of the Statement of Receipts and Disbursements or Funds Utilization Report and its liquidation with the Source Agency.  Henceforth, ensure the availability of supporting documents before disbursements are initiated.  We also recommend the reclassification of Project Equity Account in the Government Equity Account of the Trust Fund to Due to National Government Agencies, providing subsidiary ledgers for each agency.  Review of the account balances shall also be made to ensure correctness of the amounts.

We recommend that funds transferred to implementing agencies must be properly documented to ensure completion of the project, proper utilization of funds, performance of required duties and responsibilities and proper accounting and reporting of its disbursements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all depository accounts of the Municipality and submit a copy to the Auditor for timely review and verification.

We recommend that management refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advances are liquidated. Cash advances shall be fully liquidated and settled at year-end so that proper adjustments could be made on both the particular expense account and the asset account.  Also, the Municipal Accountant together with the Municipal Treasurer should closely monitor the liquidation of cash advances and strictly enforce adherence to the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 96-004 dated April 19, 1996.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the schedule of monthly remittances and facilitate remittance to concerned government agencies within the 10th day after the end of each month.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Drivers and other concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Audit Team every month for audit examination and evaluation.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should deposit her collections daily and intact and strictly adhere to the provision of Section 32 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.
